secondary_settlements: Cravenmoor's Ford (400 — contested, next Ironmark target)
Scattered farmsteads and hamlets in the Bleeding Ground (~3,350)
overview.dominant_religion: Reclaimed Path (Durn-administered territory) / Scorched Covenant (Ironmark-administered territory)
overview.dominant_faction: Kingdom of Durn (formal) / contested on the ground
overview.current_year_condition: A region that has been contested for so long that the contesting has become the landscape. The Ironmark has taken three settlements in six years through a combination of negotiation, bribery, and swift military action presented as policing. Cravenmoor's Ford is next. The Grey Contracts know it. Lord-Marshal Brennwatch knows it. The ford's council of three knows it. The intelligence that is driving Brekhard's timing is a dead Durn contingency plan sold by an asset inside the Ironmark's apparatus — Brennwatch cannot correct the misunderstanding without burning the asset and the person. The Bleeding Ground between the rivers accumulates. The Pale Company Warden watching it has compressed his estimate from years to months.
overview.danger_level: High — the most dangerous starting region. Bandits are organized. Political violence is semi-regular. Legal protection is inconsistent. The Bleeding Ground at night is in a category of its own.

the_durnmark_river.description: The western boundary of the contested zone. Aldenmere's Cross sits at its most reliable ford. Wide, brown, fast with snowmelt in Thornmelt, lower and crossable in five places during Scaldas. In Durn's administrative framework, the eastern bank is the border. The Ironmark disputes this.
the_durnmark_river.military_significance: Controlling the Durnmark crossing at Aldenmere's Cross controls movement between the Durn heartland and the eastern territories. No bridge. Three ferries, all privately operated.
the_durnmark_river.current_status: Durn-controlled. Grey Contracts patrol the western bank.

the_ashford_river.description: A smaller tributary running southeast forming the rough boundary between the contested zone and the Ironmark's acknowledged territory. Cravenmoor's Ford sits on this river.
the_ashford_river.military_significance: Whoever holds Cravenmoor's Ford controls whether Durn's eastern garrisons can be resupplied by the faster southern road or must take the three-day northern detour through terrain impassable in Thornmelt.
the_ashford_river.current_status: Nominally Durn. Effectively contested. Ironmark forward scouts have been seen on both banks.

the_bleeding_ground.description: The strip of contested land between the rivers. Roughly twenty miles wide, forty miles long. Three generations of border conflict have left it scarred. Burned farmstead foundations. Mass graves everyone knows about and nobody marks. A Hegemony-era road running north-south that both sides use and neither controls.
the_bleeding_ground.the_smell: Turned earth and, on the wrong wind, old smoke. Travelers who cross it in full dark describe the silence as wrong — the specific silence of land that has absorbed more death than it can process.

the_threshold.description: The Bleeding Ground is approaching the threshold for demonic site activation. Not there yet. Building. The accumulated death-energy of three generations of border conflict is close to sufficient. Drevhan Coldbeck of the Pale Company has been tracking this for eleven years.
the_threshold.current_estimate: Eight to twelve months before activation, accelerated by the current political escalation. Troop movements and the community trauma of contested territory are feeding the ground.
warning_signs_present: Animals crossing the Bleeding Ground at dawn and dusk are doing so faster than terrain requires
Three travelers who camped in the Bleeding Ground in the past month have reported the same dream independently — a sound like grinding stone from below the earth
The mass graves on the old battlefield site at the ground's center have begun showing surface subsidence that is not consistent with normal soil settlement
Drevhan Coldbeck's survey marks — placed to track environmental changes — show readings that are outside the eleven-year normal range
the_threshold.what_crosses_when_it_tips: Based on the Pale Company's historical records for sites with this profile — accumulated battlefield death over multiple generations, in contested ground, without ritual resolution — the initial manifestation will be ghouls within a half-mile radius of the mass grave site, followed within weeks by something larger if the site is not sealed. The 'something larger' category at this site, given the death density, will be fiend-level.

the_bleeding_ground.hegemony_road: The Brennac administrative road runs north-south through the Bleeding Ground. Better-built than anything constructed since. Both sides use it. Neither maintains it. Sections have been cannibalized for building stone in Thorngate and Weldgate, creating gaps that slow travel and expose travelers to the open ground longer than the original road allowed.

thorngate.status: Fully absorbed. Scorched Covenant temple established. Reclaimed Path wayshrine removed. The community that remains has adapted or left. The ones who adapted have learned to speak carefully.
thorngate.what_the_ironmark_did: Thorngate's minor lord accepted Ironmark administration in exchange for personal property guarantees. The guarantees held. The minor lord is now an Ironmark tax collector. The people who could not accept this left. The people who could not leave accepted it.
thorngate.notable: Three families who left Thorngate four years ago are now in Aldenmere's Cross. They are the most accurate source of information about what Ironmark absorption actually looks like from the inside.

saltfen.status: In transition. Underground Reclaimed Path congregation meets in a barn owned by a widow who has been warned twice. The Ironmark administrator is competent and not cruel, which makes the transition slower — cruelty produces resistance, and resistance clarifies loyalties. Competence produces accommodation, and accommodation is harder to reverse.
saltfen.garet_denn_origin: Garet Denn was born here. Lost his home and his tannery when Ironmark administration was established. Left. Built the Borderlands Hollow Road from what he lost.

weldgate.status: Eight months. The most recent and the most raw. The Durn garrison withdrew on capital orders that Brennwatch still considers a strategic error. Three families left the day the Ironmark soldiers arrived. The rest are watching and waiting. The Scorched Covenant has sent a Purifier to establish the faith presence. The Purifier is twenty-six years old and has never administered an absorption before. He is doing his best. His best is producing the specific community trauma that demonic influence later exploits.

weldgate_purifier.name: Edric Sorn
weldgate_purifier.note: No relation to Vael Sorn. Sorn is a common borderland surname.
weldgate_purifier.situation: Has been given authority he does not know how to use correctly. Genuinely believes in what he is doing. The specific harm he is causing is invisible to him because it accumulates slowly. Drevhan Coldbeck has visited Weldgate twice in the past month under the cartographer cover and has taken specific readings. The community trauma Edric Sorn is generating is measurably accelerating the Bleeding Ground's threshold approach.

settlements.name: Aldenmere's Cross
settlements.type: Ford crossing settlement — informal regional capital
settlements.description: Grew up around the one reliable crossing on the Durnmark River. The ford is the reason for everything here — the market exists because traders cross here, the garrison exists because Durn needs to control the crossing, the Hollow Road waypoint exists because people who need to move without documentation also need to cross rivers. The settlement has the specific quality of a place that matters more than its size suggests and knows it. The buildings are practical. The streets are mud in Thornmelt and dust in Scaldas. The smell is river water, horse, and the particular smoke of too many cook-fires too close together. Nothing is decorative. Everything is functional or it is not there.

the_ford.description: The Durnmark here is wide but shallow in two channels divided by a gravel bar. At low water — late Scaldas through early Duskarn — it is crossable on foot. At high water — Thornmelt and early Sowing — only the ferry operates. The ferry is owned by a woman named Kessa Durn who is no relation to the Kingdom of Durn, has been asked this question eight hundred times, and has a specific expression she deploys when asked it.
the_ford.military_significance: If the ford is held by force it becomes a chokepoint. If it is destroyed — which has been done twice in this region's history — the eastern supply line is severed for the duration of any Thornmelt season.

kessa_durn.disposition: Has operated this ferry for thirty years under four different administrations in the region. Her position on the current political situation is that boats float regardless of which flags are flying, and she will continue operating regardless of which flags are flying, and if anyone tries to stop her operating she will stop them with the same boat hook she used on the last three people who tried.

districts.name: The Crossing Quarter
districts.character: Immediately adjacent to the ford. The ferry dock, the toll-house, the first inn travelers reach when they cross from the east. Loud, transient, the smell of river mud and wet animal. The Hollow Road waypoint is in this quarter — in the basement of a tallow chandler's workshop whose owner has been carefully not knowing things for three years.

districts.name: The Market Row
districts.character: The commercial spine. Permanent stalls, seasonal traders, the Grey Contracts' public recruiting notice board. A blacksmith, two coopers, a tanner working from a shed that is technically too close to the well but has always been there. The Reclaimed Path temple is at the Market Row's northern end.

districts.name: The Garrison Quarter
districts.character: Brennwatch's command and the garrison troops. Old Hegemony stonework — the administrative building is Brennac-era construction that has been in continuous use for four hundred years and shows it. Better built than anything around it. The garrison's soldiers have been billeted in newer construction around it because the original Hegemony barracks were converted to storage when the population was smaller.

districts.name: The South Shore
districts.character: The poorest district. Displaced people from Thorngate, Saltfen, and Weldgate who came to Aldenmere's Cross and stayed because they had nowhere else to go. A specific grief runs through this quarter — not acute grief but the chronic kind, the grief of people who know that where they came from is still there and is simply no longer theirs.

sources: Durnmark River (primary — river water, must be boiled)
water.notes: River flooding in Thornmelt contaminates the lower wells annually. A third well was proposed four years ago and has not been dug because the funding keeps being redirected to garrison maintenance.

sources: Garrison supply chain from Durn heartland
food.notes: The garrison supply chain is what keeps Aldenmere's Cross fed in winter. If Cravenmoor's Ford falls and the southern road is disrupted, the garrison supply chain must take the northern detour, adding three days and significant cost. Food prices will rise. Brennwatch knows this. It is part of why he needs Cravenmoor's Ford.

sources: Trade from Durn's western smithing towns — three days by road
iron.notes: The Ironmark's expansion is partly about controlling the iron deposits in its eastern territories. The Borderlands itself does not produce iron — it consumes it. The garrison's equipment maintenance is running at reduced capacity because iron costs more than budget allows. Three of the garrison's soldiers have weapons that Brennwatch's quartermaster has flagged for replacement and not replaced.

settlements.name: Cravenmoor's Ford
settlements.type: River crossing settlement — contested, Brekhard's next target
settlements.location: On the Ashford River, two days southeast of Aldenmere's Cross
settlements.description: Smaller than Aldenmere's Cross and more important in the specific way of things that are about to become contested. The settlement grew around the ford on the Ashford River — the crossing that controls the southern supply road to Durn's eastern garrisons. Without it, those garrisons survive on the northern road, which adds three days and is impassable in Thornmelt. With it, they are supplied efficiently year-round. Brekhard knows this. The settlement's 400 residents know they know this.

the_council.description: No formal governance structure. Three people who have emerged as the settlement's decision-making center through different kinds of standing.

members.name: Sera Venn
members.background: Born in Cravenmoor's Ford. Left for ten years walking the Barefoot Compact circuit. Came back when her mother was dying and never left. Her faith has drifted toward the Compact's reform sensibility — less institutional, more practically focused. Can speak to Scorched Covenant officials without immediately giving offense, which is a specific skill that took effort to develop.
members.position: Do not accommodate, but do not provoke. Survive without welcoming. Preserve the difference between those two things for every family here.
members.what_she_knows: Has been in contact with the underground Reclaimed Path congregation in Saltfen. Knows what happened to communities that accommodated. Has not told the council the full picture of what accommodation looks like two years in because Wulf Carten does not want to hear it.

members.name: Wulf Carten
members.role: Trader — ford's primary trading post, council member
members.background: Has run the ford's trading post for twelve years. Serves Durn and Ironmark travelers without distinction. Considers this a business decision.
members.position: Negotiated settlement. The ford's value is the crossing, and crossings under siege are not crossings. Trade stops if this becomes a military objective regardless of who wins.
members.what_he_is_doing: Has been exploring whether a negotiated settlement is possible through channels Brennwatch does not know about — specifically, through a Saltfen trader who has maintained commercial relationships on both sides of the administration line. Has not found a path yet. Has found that the question of negotiated settlement is not unwelcome in certain Ironmark administrative circles, which he considers cautiously encouraging and which would horrify Sera Venn.

members.name: Torald Marsh
members.role: Retired Grey Contracts soldier — settlement's informal military advisor, council member
members.background: Served fourteen years with the Grey Contracts. Left when a knee injury made him a field liability. Settled here six years ago because it was the nearest settlement to where he was standing when he made the decision. Has become more this place than the company he came from.
members.physical: The knee still gives him trouble in cold weather. Walks with a slight hitch that he manages carefully. The upper body is still a soldier's — he has not let that go.
members.assessment: Cravenmoor's Ford cannot be defended against a determined Ironmark push without external reinforcement. The Grey Contracts detachment is twelve soldiers and a junior sergeant. Brennwatch's garrison is three days away. If Brekhard moves, the ford falls before relief arrives.
members.what_he_is_doing: Has organized the settlement's able-bodied residents — forty-three people — into something that could make taking the ford expensive without making it winnable. Has been doing this quietly for six weeks. Has not told Sera or Wulf that the objective is not to win but to make the cost high enough that Brekhard considers whether the ford is worth the butcher's bill right now.
members.what_he_has_not_said: He does not think it can be held. He has told the council he is preparing a defense. Both statements are true. They are not the same statement.

the_grey_contracts_detachment.commander: Junior Sergeant Bren Coldwick — 29, competent, nervous, aware that his twelve soldiers are a symbolic presence rather than a military one
the_grey_contracts_detachment.orders: Hold the ford. Report Ironmark movement. Do not engage unless directly attacked.
the_grey_contracts_detachment.what_the_orders_dont_say: What to do when the Ironmark arrives in numbers that make 'do not engage' and 'hold the ford' contradictory instructions.

active_tensions.name: The Ford Crisis
active_tensions.summary: Brekhard is moving on Cravenmoor's Ford. His timing is driven by intelligence — a dead Durn contingency plan sold through an Ironmark intelligence network — that he believes is current. Brennwatch cannot correct the misunderstanding without burning an eight-year asset and the person behind it. The ford's council of three is preparing in three incompatible directions. The Grey Contracts' position is about to become untenable.

the_intelligence_document.what_it_is: A Durn military planning document from three years ago — a contingency plan for taking the Ironmark's northern iron deposits within three years, prepared by Brennwatch's planning staff and shelved when the capital decided the cost was not worth the gain.
the_intelligence_document.how_brekhard_got_it: An asset inside the Ironmark's intelligence apparatus — a person Brennwatch has been running for eight years — sold it to an Ironmark agent six weeks ago. The asset's motivation for the sale is unknown to Brennwatch. The logical explanations are: the asset has been turned, the asset needed money, the asset is trying to provoke the confrontation for reasons Brennwatch cannot determine.
the_intelligence_document.what_brennwatch_knows: That the document is real. That it is three years old and no longer operational. That revealing its provenance requires him to acknowledge the asset's existence, which the Ironmark's intelligence apparatus would follow to the asset, who would die. He has been sitting with this for six weeks.
the_intelligence_document.the_asset: Brennwatch knows only the asset's handler's name — a woman in Durn service named Sela Vorne, who receives the intelligence through three intermediaries and forwards it to Brennwatch's desk with no identifying information. He has never met the asset. He does not know their name, their position, or their face. He knows they have been right for eight years and that they are now either a traitor or in trouble.

the_grey_contracts_position.current_contract: Border patrol for Lord-Marshal Brennwatch. Standard terms: patrol the designated line, report incursions, respond to attacks on Durn-administered settlements.
the_grey_contracts_position.the_coming_request: Brennwatch needs the Grey Contracts to hold Cravenmoor's Ford against an Ironmark advance that, if it comes in force, exceeds what twelve soldiers and a nervous sergeant can handle. Holding the ford with force is combat operations, not border patrol. It is not in the contract.
the_grey_contracts_position.sorns_position: Keeps contracts to the letter and not a word further. Has built the Grey Contracts' reputation on this. If Brennwatch asks him to hold Cravenmoor's Ford against a military advance, Sorn will tell him that is not what was contracted for and that renegotiation is possible. This conversation will happen. Both men know it is coming. Neither has initiated it yet.

player_involvement_paths: Working for Brennwatch — the most direct employment in the region, garrison auxiliary rates posted on the notice board at the Cross
Working for the Grey Contracts — their recruiting is active, the work is real, the company's position is about to become complicated
The intelligence document — finding its provenance, determining whether the asset sold it voluntarily or was compromised, and deciding what to do with that information
Cravenmoor's Ford — the council of three, the twelve soldiers, and Torald Marsh's quiet preparation
Wulf Carten's negotiation channel — not sanctioned, potentially treasonous, possibly the only thing that avoids the battle

active_tensions.name: The Bleeding Ground
active_tensions.summary: The contested strip between the rivers is approaching the threshold for demonic site activation. Drevhan Coldbeck has been tracking it for eleven years and has revised his estimate from years to months because of the current political escalation. The community trauma generated by the Ironmark's absorption process — particularly Edric Sorn's Purifier work in Weldgate — is accelerating the timeline. Nobody in Aldenmere's Cross knows this is happening.
active_tensions.drevhans_assessment: Eight to twelve months before activation. Possibly less. The Weldgate situation has produced more measurable change in the past eight weeks than the previous two years combined. The current troop movements are adding to it. If Cravenmoor's Ford becomes a battle — if a significant number of people die in the Bleeding Ground in the near future — the timeline compresses further.
active_tensions.what_activates: Initial manifestation will be ghoul emergence within a half-mile radius of the old mass grave site at the Bleeding Ground's center. Within weeks, fiend-level presence if the site is not sealed. The Scorched Covenant's Purifiers will arrive. Their methods will make it worse.
active_tensions.the_warden_and_the_soldier: Drevhan Coldbeck knows the Bleeding Ground better than anyone except Garet Denn, who moves through it regularly. Neither knows the other exists. A player who introduces these two people to each other — the retired cartographer and the displaced tanner — creates a conversation that neither could have without the introduction.
player_involvement_paths: Finding Drevhan Coldbeck in the Bleeding Ground or at his survey points
The Pale Company reputation counter — Drevhan is watching
The Weldgate Purifier situation — Edric Sorn, 26, causing measurable harm without knowing it

active_tensions.name: The Grey Contracts Internal Situation
active_tensions.summary: Brennwatch has received information suggesting a financial approach was made to someone inside the Grey Contracts. He does not know who. He has not told Sorn. Sorn can feel the specific care with which Brennwatch is handling him and does not know why. The question of the approach — whether it was made, by whom inside the company, and whether it was accepted — is a thread that runs through the Grey Contracts' one hundred and twenty soldiers currently in the Borderlands.
active_tensions.the_actual_situation: The approach was made — a Brekhard intermediary contacted a Grey Contracts sergeant named Tomas Feldwick six weeks ago and offered him a significant payment for information about the garrison's patrol schedule and strength. Feldwick has not accepted. Feldwick has not reported it to Sorn. He is waiting to see if a second approach is made before deciding whether it constitutes something he needs to act on. This is a catastrophic misjudgment that Feldwick does not know is a catastrophic misjudgment.
player_involvement_paths: Finding Tomas Feldwick and the unreported approach
Building trust with Grey Contracts soldiers over time — they talk to people they trust
Bringing the approach information to Sorn — who will have a specific and very controlled reaction

active_tensions.name: Garet Denn's Operation
active_tensions.summary: The Borderlands Hollow Road has been running for four years and has become something larger than its founder intended. Garet Denn moves displaced people, information, and small cargo. He is not professionally neutral. He will not move Ironmark agents. The broader Hollow Road has offered integration twice. He has declined twice. His operation knows the Bleeding Ground better than anyone and does not know what the ground is becoming.
active_tensions.current_stress: The current political escalation is increasing demand on Denn's operation faster than he can scale it. More families are trying to leave Weldgate. More information is moving. He needs people he can trust and is running out of them.
player_involvement_paths: Fera Colm's farmstead — the waystation, three miles east of Aldenmere's Cross
The South Shore quarter — displaced families who have used Denn's operation or know people who have
The broader Hollow Road integration question — a third offer may be coming

ambient_spawn_hooks.philosophy: The Borderlands drops characters into a place that is already moving. The hooks are more immediate than other regions — the danger is closer to the surface, the stakes of the first day are higher, the world makes it clear faster that this is not a place where nothing is happening. The tone is darker than the Amber Coast and drier than the Fens. People here speak in shorter sentences. They have learned not to say things that might need to be unsaid later.

hooks.location: Aldenmere's Cross garrison notice board, morning
hooks.hook: The Grey Contracts notice is still up. Standard terms, standard pay — two iron chits per day patrol work, three for anything that requires contact with Ironmark forces. Below the Grey Contracts notice, someone has pinned a smaller paper in rougher hand: 'Garrison auxiliary. One iron chit per day. Ask for Quartermaster Denveth.' The gap between the two rates is the gap between what the Crown can afford and what the work actually costs.
hooks.immediate_opportunity: Both employers are genuine. The Grey Contracts notice leads to Sorn's recruiting sergeant — a woman named Kela Marsh, no relation to Torald Marsh, who assesses candidates in a fifteen-minute conversation that covers combat history, prior employers, and one question that seems tangential but isn't. The garrison notice leads to a quartermaster who needs bodies on the eastern patrol and is not particular about their backgrounds.

hooks.location: The South Shore quarter, Aldenmere's Cross, arrival
hooks.hook: The South Shore is where people who came from somewhere else end up. Nobody asks about before. The specificity of not asking is itself a kind of answer — everyone here has a before they are not discussing. A woman sorting through belongings outside a shared house looks up when you pass and nods in the particular way of someone who recognizes the category without needing to know the details.
hooks.immediate_opportunity: Garet Denn's operation has a South Shore contact — a former Saltfen man named Wren Collum who identifies potential useful people for the operation. He has been watching you for ten minutes. He will not approach first. If you approach him and say anything that demonstrates you know the Bleeding Ground or have reason to move through it, he will give you a location and a time.

hooks.location: The garrison common room, Aldenmere's Cross, evening
hooks.hook: Brennwatch is eating alone at the far end of the common room, which means he is thinking and does not want to be interrupted, which every soldier in the room knows. He looks up when you come in — the specific assessment of someone who evaluates people professionally — and looks back at his food. Ten minutes later his aide, a young man named Pell Drant, appears at your table and asks if you have a moment for the Lord-Marshal.
hooks.immediate_opportunity: Brennwatch does not hire casually and does not waste time. If he has asked for you, he has a specific reason. The reason is that you carry yourself like someone who has been in service at a level above what your current circumstances suggest, and he needs someone for a task that requires that level of competence and is not something he can assign to his own men without creating questions he does not want asked.

hooks.location: The Crossing Quarter tallow chandler, Aldenmere's Cross, evening
hooks.hook: The mark is on the door lintel — not the standard mark, a borderland variant you have seen twice before, both times in contested territory. The chandler inside is a heavyset man named Osric Fenwall who looks up when you come in with the expression of someone who serves customers and is currently assessing whether you are a customer. You are not a customer. Both of you know this. He waits.
hooks.immediate_opportunity: The Borderlands Hollow Road waypoint. Osric Fenwall is not Garet Denn's operation — he predates it and operates separately, though the two have an understanding about not stepping on each other's routes. His specific need tonight is a courier run to Cravenmoor's Ford carrying a sealed packet that he will not describe. The packet is correspondence for Wulf Carten from his Saltfen contact. It is the negotiation channel that Brennwatch does not know about.

hooks.location: The Market Row, Aldenmere's Cross, Marketday morning
hooks.hook: The market has the specific quality of a market that has absorbed more people than it was built for. The South Shore displaced population has pushed the existing trader community into closer quarters and created new informal stalls in gaps between buildings. The organization of it — who is where, who has space, who is being crowded out — tells you something about the social order of the settlement that the garrison notice board does not. A woman selling preserved goods from a cart near the temple is being systematically undercut by a man two stalls over. She has noticed. He has noticed that she has noticed. Neither has acted yet.
hooks.immediate_opportunity: The woman is Bren Aldwick, a Weldgate widow eight months displaced. The man is a Durn-born market trader who has been here for twenty years and considers the influx of displaced people a commercial threat. The tension between them is the tension of the whole South Shore district in small. Bren Aldwick knows things about Weldgate's transition that the garrison's intelligence reports do not contain.

hooks.location: The Market Row smithy, Aldenmere's Cross, morning
hooks.hook: The smith — a large, quiet man named Cavan Dalt — is working on garrison equipment. Not new equipment. Old equipment that should have been replaced two years ago. The repair work he is doing on a soldier's sword is the specific kind of repair that is better than nothing and worse than replacement, and he knows it, and he is doing it anyway because replacement is not in the garrison's current budget. He notices you watching and says nothing. The smith who does not tell you to get out is telling you something.
hooks.immediate_opportunity: The garrison's equipment maintenance problem. Cavan Dalt needs an extra hand and cannot afford to hire one at market rates. He will take barter — work for work. The garrison equipment problem is Brennwatch's quiet shame and his most concrete vulnerability if the situation with Brekhard escalates to actual combat.

hooks.location: The Crossing Quarter, Aldenmere's Cross, night
hooks.hook: The ferry is not running at night. The official reason is visibility and river safety. The actual reason is that Kessa Durn charges double for night crossings and only operates them for people who knock on a specific door and say a specific phrase, which costs one bronze mark to learn and which you have just paid for from a boy near the toll-house who charged you in advance and whom you will never see again. The phrase works. Kessa Durn opens the door, looks at you, and says: 'East or west?'
hooks.immediate_opportunity: Kessa Durn's night ferry is one of three unofficial crossing operations on the Durnmark. The other two are less reliable and more dangerous. Her operation serves people who need to cross without being recorded at the toll. Her passenger list — which she keeps in her head rather than on paper — is one of the most valuable pieces of intelligence in the Borderlands, which is why three separate organizations have tried to access it and all three have been declined with the same boat hook.

hooks.location: The South Shore, Aldenmere's Cross, morning
hooks.hook: A child is sick in the South Shore — one of the Weldgate displaced families. The mother has been to the garrison's medical orderly, who gave her a remedy that is not working, and to the Reclaimed Path temple, where Sister Morna Daelwick gave her what the temple had and told her she needed someone with more knowledge. The mother is now standing in the street asking anyone who passes.
hooks.immediate_opportunity: The child's illness is straightforward — treatable with the right materials. The South Shore has neither the materials nor the knowledge. A healer who treats this child earns the trust of the entire South Shore displaced community within hours, because that community is very small and very tight and the information about who helped moves through it instantly. The South Shore community has information about the three taken towns, Garet Denn's operation, and the current state of the Bleeding Ground from people who have crossed it recently.

hooks.location: The garrison administrative building, Aldenmere's Cross, morning
hooks.hook: The Hegemony administrative building has a records room that the garrison uses for storage. The records room contains four hundred years of Brennac administrative documents that nobody has catalogued because nobody has had time. A note on the door says 'records access by application to the quartermaster.' The application process is a form. The form asks your name, your scholarly affiliation, and the specific documents you are seeking. The third question is the interesting one — there are documents in that room that several parties would pay significantly to access, and the garrison does not know what they are.
hooks.immediate_opportunity: The Hegemony records contain two things of specific current relevance: pre-Reckoning survey documents for the Bleeding Ground area that Drevhan Coldbeck does not know exist, and correspondence from the period of the last serious demonic site activation in this region — approximately three hundred years ago — that describes what the activation looked like and what resolved it. Both of these are in the room. Neither has been read in at least a century.

hooks.location: The road south of Aldenmere's Cross toward Cravenmoor's Ford, dawn
hooks.hook: You were moving a small flock south — work you picked up from a farm three days back — and the sheep stopped at a specific point on the road. Not a fence, not a sound, not any stimulus you can identify. The whole flock simply stopped and refused to move east. You have been moving sheep for long enough to know the difference between sheep being stupid and sheep responding to something. You are looking at the eastern ground and seeing nothing wrong with it. The sheep are looking at the same ground and disagreeing.
hooks.immediate_opportunity: The specific point where the sheep stopped is on the Bleeding Ground's western edge. Animals reading the pre-activation ground as hostile is one of Drevhan Coldbeck's documented warning signs. A shepherd who reports this to the right person — Drevhan, or a player who has encountered other warning signs — adds a data point to the threshold picture.

hooks.location: The South Shore, Aldenmere's Cross, arrival
hooks.hook: You walked in from the east, which means you came through Weldgate or near it. The woman at the South Shore community well asks where you came from with the specific interest of someone who has people there. When you say Weldgate, she stops drawing water and gives you her complete attention.
hooks.immediate_opportunity: Eight months of Ironmark administration in Weldgate as seen by someone who just came through it. The South Shore wants this information. Garet Denn's South Shore contact Wren Collum also wants this information and is willing to pay for accurate intelligence from someone who has been in Weldgate recently.

hooks.location: The farmlands east of Aldenmere's Cross, morning
hooks.hook: You found work on a farm three miles east of the Cross — seasonal labor, honest rate. The farm belongs to a widow named Fera Colm. The first day of work involves being shown where the barn is and being told very specifically that the barn is not your business. The barn is locked with a better lock than any other lock on the property. The widow notices you noticing this and holds your gaze for a moment before continuing the tour.
hooks.immediate_opportunity: Fera Colm's farmstead is Garet Denn's waystation. The locked barn is the operation. Working this farm means proximity to the Hollow Road's most active node in the Borderlands. Fera Colm will test a new worker for two weeks before deciding whether to acknowledge what the barn is for.
